query_normalized: exploring the impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by investigating the same core mechanism (multiple imputation methods) and its validity conditions, which is a prerequisite for assessing their impact on downstream statistical inference, even though it focuses on identifiability theory rather than a specific causal estimator. It also meets criterion (b) by establishing the theoretical baseline for when imputation methods are valid, a critical component for any literature review on imputation in causal inferenc
